details.description: Abrocitinib might increase the exposure to Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Both Ciclosporin and Aceclofenac can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.

Both Ciclosporin and Aceclofenac can increase the risk of hyperkalaemia (hyperkalaemia is particularly notable when ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II receptor antagonists are given with spironolactone or eplerenone).
Both Ciclosporin and Aciclovir can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Afatinib. Manufacturer advises separating administration—consult product literature.
Ciclosporin markedly increases the exposure to Aliskiren. Manufacturer advises avoid.

Both Ciclosporin and Aliskiren can increase the risk of hyperkalaemia (hyperkalaemia is particularly notable when ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II receptor antagonists are given with spironolactone or eplerenone).
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Alpelisib. Manufacturer advises caution and monitor for toxicity.
Ciclosporin moderately increases the exposure to Ambrisentan. Manufacturer advises adjust Ambrisentan dose and monitor.
Both Ciclosporin and Amikacin can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Amphotericin B can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Apalutamide decreases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Apixaban. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Aprepitant is predicted to increase the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Both Ciclosporin and Aspirin can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Atazanavir increases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Atogepant. Manufacturer advises adjust Atogepant dose.
Ciclosporin markedly to very markedly increases the exposure to Atorvastatin. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust Atorvastatin dose.
Bacillus Calmette-Guérin vaccine is predicted to increase the risk of generalised infection (possibly life-threatening) when given with Ciclosporin. UKHSA advises avoid (refer to Green Book).
Baricitinib is predicted to enhance the risk of immunosuppression when given with Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution or avoid—consult product literature.
Bezafibrate is predicted to increase the risk of nephrotoxicity when given with Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises monitor renal function.
Blinatumomab is predicted to transiently increase the exposure to Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Bosentan moderately decreases the exposure to Ciclosporin and Ciclosporin moderately increases the exposure to Bosentan. Manufacturer advises avoid.
Ciclosporin increases the risk of neutropenia when given with Brentuximab vedotin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Brigatinib might decrease the exposure to Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises avoid.

Both Ciclosporin and Bromfenac can increase the risk of hyperkalaemia (hyperkalaemia is particularly notable when ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II receptor antagonists are given with spironolactone or eplerenone).
Ciclosporin is predicted to affect the efficacy of Bulevirtide and Bulevirtide is predicted to increase the exposure to Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises avoid.
Cannabidiol is predicted to increase the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Capivasertib. Manufacturer advises adjust dose.
Both Ciclosporin and Captopril can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.

Both Ciclosporin and Captopril can increase the risk of hyperkalaemia (hyperkalaemia is particularly notable when ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II receptor antagonists are given with spironolactone or eplerenone).
Carbamazepine decreases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Both Ciclosporin and Carboplatin can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Ciclosporin slightly increases the exposure to Caspofungin. Manufacturer advises monitor liver function.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefaclor can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefadroxil can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefalexin can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefazolin can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefepime can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefixime can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefotaxime can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefoxitin can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Cefradine can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Ceftazidime can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Ceftobiprole can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Both Ciclosporin and Ceftriaxone can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.

Both Ciclosporin and Celecoxib can increase the risk of hyperkalaemia (hyperkalaemia is particularly notable when ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II receptor antagonists are given with spironolactone or eplerenone).
Ceritinib increases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciclosporin is predicted to affect the efficacy of Chenodeoxycholic acid. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Cholera vaccine (live) is predicted to increase the risk of generalised infection (possibly life-threatening) when given with Ciclosporin. UKHSA advises avoid (refer to Green Book).
Ciclosporin affects the concentration of Cholic acid. Manufacturer advises avoid.
Both Ciclosporin and Cidofovir can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Cimetidine increases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution and monitor renal function.
Both Ciclosporin and Cisplatin can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Cladribine. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose.
Clarithromycin increases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Cobicistat increases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciclosporin increases the exposure to Colchicine. Manufacturer advises avoid P-glycoprotein inhibitors or adjust Colchicine dose.
Crizotinib is predicted to increase the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the risk of rhabdomyolysis when given with Daptomycin. Manufacturer advises monitor.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Daridorexant. Manufacturer advises adjust Daridorexant dose.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Darifenacin. Manufacturer advises avoid.
Darunavir increases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciclosporin increases the concentration of Daunorubicin. Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Both Ciclosporin and Deferasirox can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.
Dengue vaccine is predicted to increase the risk of generalised infection (possibly life-threatening) when given with Ciclosporin. UKHSA advises avoid (refer to Green Book).
Both Ciclosporin and Dexketoprofen can increase the risk of nephrotoxicity.

Both Ciclosporin and Dexketoprofen can increase the risk of hyperkalaemia (hyperkalaemia is particularly notable when ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II receptor antagonists are given with spironolactone or eplerenone).

Ciclosporin increases the concentration of Digoxin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Ciclosporin increases the concentration of Docetaxel (oral). Manufacturer makes no recommendation.
Doxycycline is predicted to increase the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises monitor.
Dronedarone is predicted to increase the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.

Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Elacestrant. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust Elacestrant dose.
Elranatamab might affect the exposure to Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises monitor and adjust dose.
Ciclosporin causes a small decrease in the exposure to Eltrombopag. Manufacturer advises monitor platelet count and adjust dose.
Encorafenib decreases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Enzalutamide decreases the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.

Both Ciclosporin and Etoricoxib can increase the risk of hyperkalaemia (hyperkalaemia is particularly notable when ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II receptor antagonists are given with spironolactone or eplerenone).
Etravirine is predicted to decrease the exposure to Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution.
Ciclosporin moderately increases the exposure to Everolimus. Manufacturer advises avoid or adjust dose.
Ciclosporin moderately increases the exposure to Ezetimibe and Ezetimibe slightly increases the exposure to Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution and monitor Ciclosporin concentration.
Fenofibrate increases the risk of nephrotoxicity when given with Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises monitor renal function.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Fidaxomicin. Manufacturer advises avoid.
Filgotinib is predicted to increase the risk of immunosuppression when given with Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises avoid.

Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the concentration of Ranolazine and Ranolazine is predicted to increase the concentration of Ciclosporin. Manufacturer advises caution and monitor Ciclosporin concentration.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Relugolix. Manufacturer advises avoid or take relugolix first and separate administration by at least 6 hours.

Ciclosporin slightly increases the exposure to Rimegepant. Manufacturer advises avoid another dose of rimegepant within 48 hours of concurrent use.
Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the exposure to Riociguat. Manufacturer advises caution and monitor blood pressure.

Ritlecitinib is predicted to increase the exposure to Ciclosporin and Ciclosporin is predicted to increase the risk of generalised infection (possibly life-threatening) when given with Ritlecitinib. Manufacturer advises avoid.
